titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Laura McPhee Papers
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 16482706.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 3, 2024 at 04:25

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Photographic material including film negatives and contact proofs, gelatin silver photographic prints, and color photographic prints; professional papers including teaching and project files; correspondence; personal papers; and printed materials by or relating to photographer Laura McPhee.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 138-139 (financial records): Restricted until January 1, 2050. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Boxes 185-188 (personal correspondence): Restricted until after the death of John McPhee. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Laura McPhee on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund, 2023.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Photographic Material, 1970-2021. II. Professional Papers, 1960s-2022. III. Correspondence, 1982-2021. IV. Personal Papers, 1850s-2014. V. Printed Material, 1976-2022.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Laura McPhee (born 1958) is an American photographer. She is the daughter of literary journalist John McPhee (born 1931) and his first wife, photographer Pryde Brown (born 1935). She is also the sister of novelist Jenny McPhee, novelist Martha McPhee, and architectural historian Sarah McPhee. Following Pryde Brown's marriage to psychologist Dan Sullivan in 1969, the McPhee children grew up on a farm outside of Princeton, New Jersey, with their younger half-sister, Joan Sullivan, and their stepfather's children from his first marriage.

McPhee graduated with a Bachelor of Arts in Art History from Princeton University in 1980, and a Master of Fine Arts in Photography from the Rhode Island School of Design in 1986. Her work, which ranges from portrait to landscape to still life, is widely exhibited nationally and internationally. Her monographs and other books include No Ordinary Land (1998), Girls: Ordinary Girls And Their Extraordinary Pursuits (2000), River Of No Return (2008), Guardians Of Solitude (2009), Gateway: Visions For An Urban National Park (2011), and The Home And The World: A View Of Calcutta (2014).

She has been a member of the faculty at the Massachusetts College of Art and Design since 1986. She has been the recipient of a number of grants and residencies, including a Fulbright Scholars Fellowship for work in India and Sri Lanka, and a residency in Idaho from the Alturas Foundation. She was also awarded a New England Foundation for the Arts fellowship and a John Simon Guggenheim Memorial Foundation fellowship.
McPhee married experimental filmmaker, documentarian, and teacher Mark LaPore (1952-2005), with whom she had a daugher, Isobel McPhee. Laura McPhee lives in Brookline, Massachusetts.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Laura McPhee Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related material: Laura McPhee Photographs of the American West (WA Photos Folio 152), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.
Related material: Laura McPhee River of No Return Collection (WA Photos 591), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.
Related material: Martha McPhee Papers (YCAL MSS 1606), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographic material including film negatives and contact proofs, gelatin silver photographic prints, and color photographic prints; professional papers including teaching and project files; correspondence; personal papers; and printed materials by or relating to photographer Laura McPhee.

Collection materials document McPhee's artistic processes while travelling and photographing China, Florida, France, India, Maine, Morocco, New Jersey, New York, Sri Lanka, and the American West (including Arizona, California, Idaho, New Mexico, Nevada, Oregon, Utah, and Wyoming), among other locations.

Also documented are McPhee's relationships with her family including her sisters Jenny McPhee, Martha McPhee, Sarah McPhee, and Joan Sullivan, mother Pryde Brown, father John McPhee, husband Mark Lapore, daughter Isobel McPhee, and others.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Laura McPhee Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

geogname.part: Gateway National Recreation Area (N.J. and N.Y.)

corpname.part: Massachusetts College of Art and Design

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into four subseries: Film Negatives and Contact Proofs, Gelatin Silver Prints, Color Prints, and Digital Color Prints.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Within each project, Laura McPhee's original arrangement has been retained.
In instances where some or all captions are present, prints are listed at the item level. In instances where no identifying information is present, prints are described briefly in the aggregate.
Information in brackets has been transcribed from film negative and contact proof sleeves.
When present with prints, negative numbers are included in parentheses.
McPhee used varying numbering schemes both within and across projects. There is no documentation within the collection to provide additional context.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains photographic material (film negatives and contact proofs, gelatin silver prints, color prints, and digital color prints) created by Laura McPhee.

Individuals with formal and informal portraits include Chistina Beahan, Pryde Brown, Katherine Brown, Melissa DeWitte, Nicholas Gilson, Anya Hurlbert, Madge King, Mark LaPore, Isobel McPhee, Jenny McPhee, John McPhee, Laura McPhee, Martha McPhee, Sarah McPhee, Stephen Murray, Luca Passaleva, Elizabeth Sawyer, Sandro Stille, Dan Sullivan, Danny Sullivan, Joan Sullivan, Mary Sullivan, Merle Sullivan, Tony Sullivan, and Mark Svenvold.

Among the locations depicted are China, Florida, France, India, Maine, Morocco, New Jersey, New York, Sri Lanka, and the American West (including Arizona, California, Idaho, New Mexico, Nevada, Oregon, Utah, and Wyoming).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by project title assigned by McPhee with family film negatives and contact proofs filed at the end.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: During processing, film negatives and contact proofs were removed from binders with snap locks and rehoused into archival boxes.
Project titles established by Laura McPhee were maintained. Additional titles or annotations (if present) are included in brackets following negative numbers.
The original arrangement within each binder has been maintained and each box contains the negatives and contact proofs (if present) from only one project.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Professional Papers: Project Files: "Frank Stella job"
See also: Professional Papers: Project Files: Girls: Ordinary Girls and Their Extraordinary Pursuits : Photographic Material
See also: Professional Papers: Project Files: " Travel & Leisure Hawaii job"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Film negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee, 1970-2021.

Individuals depicted include Chistina Beahan, Pryde Brown, Katherine Brown, Melissa DeWitte, Nicholas Gilson, Anya Hurlbert, Madge King, Mark LaPore, Isobel McPhee, Jenny McPhee, John McPhee, Laura McPhee, Martha McPhee, Sarah McPhee, Stephen Murray, Luca Passaleva, Elizabeth Sawyer, Sandro Stille, Dan Sullivan, Danny Sullivan, Joan Sullivan, Mary Sullivan, Merle Sullivan, Tony Sullivan, and Mark Svenvold.
Among the locations depicted are China, Florida, France, Idaho, India, Maine, Morocco, Nevada, New Jersey, New York, Sri Lanka, and Utah.
Approximately 2,507 21 x 26 cm film negatives and 6,231 6 x 6 cm film negatives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee at the Brooklyn Navy Yard (also known as the New York Naval Shipyard), 2005.
Negatives numbered 1120-1142 are color negatives on Kodak Portra 400 Natural Color (NC) film, 6 x 6 cm.
Negatives numbered 3416-3445 are color negatives on Kodak Portra 400 Natural Color (NC) film, 26 x 21 cm.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Color Prints: Calcutta
See also: Digital Color Prints: Calcutta

scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee in Calcutta (also known as Kolkata), 1998-2013.

scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee in Calcutta (also known as Kolkata), 1998.
All negatives are color negatives on Kodak Portra 400 Natural Color (NC) film, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee in Calcutta (also known as Kolkata), 1998-2001.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee in Calcutta (also known as Kolkata), 2013.
All negatives are color negatives on Kodak Portra 400 Natural Color (NC) film, 26 x 21 cm.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two headings: Black-and-white Film Negatives and Color Film Negatives.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Digital Color Prints: Driveway Portraits

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee in Calcutta (also known as Kolkata), 1998-2013.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All negatives are black-and-white negatives, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All negatives are color negatives on Kodak film, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee in Calcutta (also known as Kolkata), 2001-2005.
Black-and-white and color negatives, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images appearing in Laura McPhee's The Home and the World: A View of Calcutta (New Haven, Connecticut: Yale University Press, 2014).
All negatives are color negatives on Kodak Portra 400 Natural Color (NC) film, 26 x 21 cm.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: During processing, negatives and contact proofs identified as "US West" were separated.
For negatives and contact proofs of US West: Negatives and Contact Proofs: West

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee in Calcutta (also known as Kolkata), 2013-2014.
All negatives are color negatives, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee in Calcutta (also known as Kolkata) and Morocco, undated.
All negatives are color negatives, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee in China at an unidentified date.
All negatives are color negatives on Kodak Portra 400 Natural Color (NC) film, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee while a student at Princeton University, 1977-1979.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives on Kodak film, 6 x 6 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of Gateway National Recreation Area in New York City and Monmouth County, New Jersey, 2011.
Some images appear in Gateway: Visions for an Urban National Park (New York: Princeton Architectural Press, 2011).
All negatives are color negatives on Kodak Portra 400 Natural Color (NC) film, 26 x 21 cm.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Color Prints: Guardians of Solitude
See also: Digital Color Prints: Guardians of Solitude

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee in three canyons in the White Cloud Mountains of central Idaho, 2008.
Some images appear in Guardians of Solitude (London: Iris Editions, 2009).
All negatives are color negatives on Kodak Portra 400 Natural Color (NC) film, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee in Idaho, 2011.
All negatives are color negatives on Kodak Portra 400 Natural Color (NC) film, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of Idaho and Nevada, 2012.
All negatives are color negatives, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of her daughter Isobel McPhee, 2004-2013.
Negatives are chiefly color negatives on Kodak Portra 400 Natural Color (NC) film, 26 x 21 cm.
Negatives titled "Idaho 2010" and "Swimming pool" are color negatives on Kodak Portra 400 Natural Color (NC) film, 6 x 6 cm.

did.unittitle: No negative numbers [Idaho 2010, Swimming pool, and miscellaneous]

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives created by Laura McPhee of trees and landscapes in unidentified locations, 2021. Identified by McPhee as "New Work."
All negatives are color negatives, 26 x 21 cm.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two headings: Black-and-white Film Negatives and Color Film Negatives.

scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of Sri Lankan tea pluckers, approximately 1990.

scopecontent.p: All negatives are black-and-white negatives, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All negatives are color negatives on Kodak film, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives for images created by Laura McPhee of street scenes and landscapes in unidentified locations, summer 2018-2019.
All negatives are color negatives, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee in Utah, 2010-2012.
All negatives are color negatives on Kodak Portra 400 Natural Color (NC) film, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of the American West, 2013-2017.
All negatives are color negatives, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee while traveling in Europe and Asia, 1977-1981.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives on Kodak film, 6 x 6 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs created by Laura McPhee, 1977-1981. Identified by McPhee as "Miscellaneous."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of of family and friends, 1970-1993.
Negatives are chiefly black-and-white negatives on Kodak film, 6 x 6 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of family and friends while in middle school and high school, 1970-1976.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives on Kodak film, 6 x 6 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of family while at Princeton University, 1976-1980.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives on Kodak film, 6 x 6 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of friends while at Princeton University, 1976-1980.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives on Kodak film, 6 x 6 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee while traveling, 1980-1992.
Identified locations include California, Egypt, Germany, Hawaii, Massachusetts, New Zealand, Rome, Sri Lanka, and the United Kingdom.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives on Kodak film, 6 x 6 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of family and friends at Omega Farm in Hunterdon County, New Jersey, 1982-1992.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives on Kodak film, 6 x 6 cm.

did.unittitle: No negative numbers [Joan, Merle, Grammy, Mom]

scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of family while a graduate student at the Rhode Island School of Design, 1984-1986.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives on Kodak film, 6 x 6 cm.

did.unittitle: No negative numbers [Summer, Jenny, Scott, Juan, Merle]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of friends while a graduate student at the Rhode Island School of Design, 1984-1986.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives on Kodak film, 6 x 6 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of family and friends in Brookline, Cambridge, and Somerville, Massachusetts, 1990-1991.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives on Kodak film, 6 x 6 cm.

did.unittitle: No negative numbers [Jeremy and Jonathan, Cambridge]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of family and friends in Belmont, Massachusetts, 1988-1989.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives on Kodak film, 6 x 6 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of family and friends in the Berkshires, Massachusetts, Block Island, Rhode Island, and Maine, 1984-1992.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives on Kodak film, 6 x 6 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee family and friends in New York City, 1987-1989.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives on Kodak film, 6 x 6 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of her stepfather Dan Sullivan, 1990-1993.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives, 6 x 6 cm and 21 x 26 cm.

scopecontent.head: Content Warning
scopecontent.p: Multiple negatives and contact proofs show Dan Sullivan on his deathbed and potentially postmortem.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of family and friends.
All negatives are black-and-white negatives, 26 x 21 cm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives and contact proofs created by Laura McPhee of ranunculus (flowers) and possibly the American West, 1998-2001.
Materials in this grouping do not appear to have a connection with materials elsewhere in the Negatives and Contact Proofs subseries.
All negatives are color negatives, 26 x 21 cm.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically with family photographs filed at the end.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Within each project, Laura McPhee's original arrangement has been retained.

In instances where some or all captions or image numbers are present, prints are listed at the item level. In instances where no identifying information is present, prints are described in the aggregate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gelatin silver prints created by Laura McPhee, approximately 1972-1994.

Individuals depicted include Chistina Beahan, Pryde Brown, Katherine Brown, Melissa DeWitte, Nicholas Gilson, Anya Hurlbert, Madge King, Mark LaPore, Jenny McPhee, Laura McPhee, Martha McPhee, Sarah McPhee, Stephen Murray, Luca Passaleva, Elizabeth Sawyer, Sandro Stille, Dan Sullivan, Danny Sullivan, Joan Sullivan, Mary Sullivan, Merle Sullivan, Tony Sullivan, and Mark Svenvold.
Locations depicted include Asia, New Jersey, New York, and Rhode Island.
Approximately 489 gelatin silver prints, 56 x 51 cm and smaller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gelatin silver prints created by taken by Laura McPhee while a student at Solebury School in New Hope, Pennsylvania, approximately 1972-1976.
Depicted are unidentified men, women, and children.
Fourteen gelatin silver prints, 26 x 20 cm and smaller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gelatin silver prints created by by Laura McPhee as part of her first semester photo portfolio at Princeton University, 1976.
Depicted are unidentified women (presumably classmates).
Twelve gelatin silber prints, 11 x 8 cm and smaller, on mounts 26 x 20 cm. Twenty-one cutouts from duplicate photographs are also present.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gelatin silver prints created by Laura McPhee while an undergraduate student at Princeton University, 1976-1980.
Depicted are unidentified men, women, and children.
One hundred twenty-four gelatin silver prints, 35.5 x 28 cm and smaller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gelatin silver prints created by Laura McPhee of unidentified individuals in Belaga, Malaysia; Mandalay, Myanmar; Nepal; and other unidentified locations, 1980-1981.
Twenty gelatin silver prints, 35.5 x 28 cm and smaller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gelatin silver prints created by Laura McPhee of family and friends while a graduate student at the Rhode Island School of Design and after, approximately 1984-1987.

Among those depicted are Chistina Beahan, Pryde Brown, Katherine Brown, Melissa DeWitte, Nicholas Gilson, Anya Hurlbert, Madge King, Mark LaPore, Jenny McPhee, Laura McPhee, Martha McPhee, Sarah McPhee, Stephen Murray, Luca Passaleva, Elizabeth Sawyer, Sandro Stille, Dan Sullivan, Danny Sullivan, Joan Sullivan, Mary Sullivan, Merle Sullivan, Tony Sullivan, and Mark Svenvold.
Seventy-three gelatin silver prints, 35.5 x 28 cm and smaller.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Negatives and Contact Proofs: Family

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gelatin silver prints created by Laura McPhee of her family and friends, approximately 1977-1994.

Among those depicted are Pryde Brown, Anya Hurlbert, Mark LaPore, Jenny McPhee, Laura McPhee, Martha McPhee, Sarah McPhee, Dan Sullivan, Danny Sullivan, Joan Sullivan, Merle Sullivan, and Mark Svenvold, as well as "Barabara," "Beth," and "Mary."
Two hundred twenty-five gelatin silver prints, 56 x 51 cm and smaller.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title assigned by Laura McPhee with "Early color work" filed at the beginning.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Within each project, Laura McPhee's original arrangement has been retained.

In instances where some or all captions or image numbers are present, prints are listed at the item level. In instances where no identifying information is present, prints are described in the aggregate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color prints created by Laura McPhee, approximately 1978-2019.
Prints are of Laura McPhee and family, China, Florida, France, Kolkata, Sri Lankan tea pluckers, and the White Cloud Mountains in central Idaho.
Two hundred sixty-six color prints, 51 x 61 cm and smaller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color prints created by Laura McPhee of family and friends, aproximately 1978.
Among those depicted are Pryde Brown, Laura McPhee, Joan Sullivan, Merle Sullivan, and "Isabelle."
Twenty-one color prints, 35.5 x 28 cm and smaller.

title.part: Aftermath

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Color Prints: Guardians of Solitude
See also: Digital Color Prints: Guardians of Solitude

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color prints created by Laura McPhee of the aftermath of forest wildfires in the White Cloud Mountains of central Idaho, 2007.
"Aftermath" is an earlier version of "Guardians of Solitude."
Ten color prints, 51 x 61 cm.

did.unittitle: Aftermath #1, Fourth of July Canyon, White Cloud Mountains, Idaho (Neg #92864)
Creek Bottom, Looking North, White Cloud Mountains, Idaho (Neg #92869)
Firewood Gone to Seed Amidst Aspens, Fourth of July Canyon, White Cloud Mountains, Idaho (Neg #92873)
Felled Trees and Shadows, Fisher Creek Canyon, White Cloud Mountains, Idaho (Neg #92860)
Regrowth After Wildfire on a Closed Road Near Aztec Mine, White Cloud Mountains, Idaho (Neg # 92854)

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Image captions were transcribed. Laura McPhee used both Calcutta and Kolkata.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Negatives and Contact Proofs: Calcutta
See also: Digital Color Prints: Calcutta

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color prints created by Laura McPhee of Kolkata, the former capital of India, 1998-2009.
Depicted are courtyards, living spaces, temples, and altars, as well as street scenes in both North Kolkata and South Kolkata.
Some images appear in McPhee's The Home and the World: A View of Calcutta (Yale University Press, 2014).
One hundred twenty-six color prints, 51 x 61 cm and smaller.

title.part: Aloke Krishna Deb, Sovabazar Rajbari, North Kolkata
Side Entrance, Mitra House, North Kolkata
Colonnade, Mitra House, North Kolkata
Rama Krishna, Deb House, Sovabazar Rajbari, North Kolkata
Durga Puja, Rani Rashmani, Bari, Kolkata
Dispensary at B. K. Paul Chemist Shop, Sovabazar, North Kolkata
Boys Playing Carom, Seth Ram Kumar Bangur Dharamshala, North Kolkata
Two-Hundred-and-Thirty-Year-Old Gods, Shri Sonar Gouranga Temple, North Kolkata
Fresh Fish, Lake Market, Kolkata
Offerings, Dari Ka Bhavan, North Kolkata
Puja at Mudiali Pandal, Kolkata
Durga Puja, Dari Ka Bhavan, North Kolkata
Durga Puja at Hatkhola Dutt Bari, North Kolkata

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Negatives and Contact Proofs: China

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color prints created by Laura McPhee in China at an unknown date.
Depicted are exteriors and street scenes in unidentified locations.
Ten color prints, 44 x 56 cm.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Negatives and Contact Proofs: Florida

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color prints created by Laura McPhee of Florida at an unknown date.
Depicted are landscapes in unidentified locations.
Two color prints, 51 x 61 cm.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Negatives and Contact Proofs: France

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color prints created by Laura McPhee of France, 2007.
Depicted are landscapes and street scenes in unidentified locations.
Forty-five color prints, 44 x 56 cm.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Negatives and Contact Proofs: Gateway
See also: Digital Color Prints: Gateway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color prints created by Laura McPhee of Gateway National Recreation Area in New York City and Monmouth County, New Jersey, 2011.
Images appear in Gateway: Visions for an Urban National Park (New York: Princeton Architectural Press, 2011).
Two color prints, 51 x 61 cm.

title.part: Guardians of Solitude

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Negatives and Contact Proofs: Guardians of Solitude
See also: Digital Color Prints: Guardians of Solitude

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color prints created by Laura McPhee of three canyons in the White Cloud Mountains of central Idaho, 2019.
Some images appear in McPhee's Guardians of Solitude (London: Iris Editions, 2009).
Thirty-six color prints, 28 x 35.5 cm.

did.unittitle: 94104, 94089, No number, 94216, No number, No number

94152, No number, 94091, 94096, No number, No number

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Negatives and Contact Proofs: Sri Lanka Tea Pluckers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color prints created by Laura McPhee of Sri Lankan tea pluckers, approximately 1990.
Individuals depicted are unidentified.
Fourteen color prints, 51 x 61 cm.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title assigned by Laura McPhee.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Within each project, Laura McPhee's original arrangement has been retained.

In instances where some or all captions or image numbers are present, prints are listed at the item level. In instances where no identifying information is present, prints are described in the aggregate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Digital color prints created by Laura McPhee, 1998-2018.
Prints are of Kolkata, the American West (including Arizona, California, Idaho, New Mexico, Nevada, Oregon, Utah, and Wyoming), and McPhee's daughter Isobel McPhee.
Four hundred thirty-eight digital color prints, 51 x 61 cm and smaller.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Image captions were transcribed. Laura McPhee used both Calcutta and Kolkata.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Negatives and Contact Proofs: Calcutta
See also: Color Prints: Calcutta

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Digital color prints created by Laura McPhee of Kolkata, the former capital of India, 1998-2013.
Depicted are courtyards, living spaces, temples, and altars, as well as street scenes in both North Kolkata and South Kolkata.
Some images appear in McPhee's The Home and the World: A View of Calcutta (Yale University Press, 2014).
Seventy-two digital color prints, 44 x 56 cm.

title.part: Ms. Pal's Private Room, Pal House, North Kolkata
Sen's Thakurbari (House of Worship) Raja Rammohan Sarani / Amherst Street, North Kolkata

Desert Chronicle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Digital color prints created by Laura McPhee of what she identifies as "the New West," 2004-2018.
Chiefly depicted are landscapes, some showing extractive industries in the American West.
Identified locations include Arizona, California, Idaho, New Mexico, Nevada, Oregon, Utah, and Wyoming.
Two hundred twenty-six digital color prints, 44 x 56 cm.

did.unittitle: Breached Dam at Sunbeam, Salmon River, Custer County, Idaho (NEG#94721)

The Castle, Capitol Reef, Utah

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Negatives and Contact Proofs: Calcutta: Calcutta Driveway Portraits

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Digital color prints created by Laura McPhee of men, women, and children in the same driveway in Jodhpur Park, Kolkata, 1998.
Some images appear in McPhee's The Home and the World: A View of Calcutta (Yale University Press, 2014).
Twenty digital color prints, 51 x 61 cm and smaller.

title.part: Angana Bomik with Her Drawing, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata
Veena, Night Servant from Bangladesh, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata
Devotees of Shiva, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata
Rag Pickers with One-Stringed Instruments, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata
Manna, an Ayah, with her two charges, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata
Ganesh Das, Ice Cream Man, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata
Gopol Mondol Selling Live Crabs, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata
Street Performer with Two Monkeys, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata
Grandmother and Granddaughter, Tibetan Refugees, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata
Rag Pickers with One-Stringed Instruments, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata
Gopol Mondol Selling Live Crabs, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata
Samir Selling Parakeets and Rabbits, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata
Dilip Das, Rickshaw Driver, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata
Pool Singh Selling Brooms, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata
Sarada Selling Stainless, Jodhpur Park, Kolkata

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Negatives and Contact Proofs: Gateway
See also: Color Prints: Gateway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Digital color prints created by Laura McPhee of Gateway National Recreation Area in New York City and Monmouth County, New Jersey, approximately 2011.
Some images appear in Gateway: Visions for an Urban National Park (New York: Princeton Architectural Press, 2011).
Fifty-four digital color prints, 28 x 35.5 cm and smaller.

Quarters A, Commanding Officer's House, US Navy, Floyd Bennett Field
Marine Forces Reserve, Floyd Bennett Field

did.unittitle: '68 Chevelle, Raptor Point, Floyd Bennett Field, Gate (Proof #1)

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Negatives and Contact Proofs: Guardians of Solitude
See also: Color Prints: Guardians of Solitude

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Digital color prints created by Laura McPhee of three canyons in the White Cloud Mountains of central Idaho, undated.
Some images appear in McPhee's Guardians of Solitude (London: Iris Editions, 2009).
Forty-four digital color prints, 44 x 56 cm.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Negatives and Contact Proofs: Isobel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Digital color prints created by Laura McPhee of her daughter Isobel McPhee at an unidentified date.
Several prints are of unidentified children, as well as of flowers.
Approximately 22 digital color prints, 51 x 61 cm and smaller.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into five subseries: Financial Records, Massachusetts College of Art and Design, Project Files, Other Papers and Realia, and Works by Others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains Laura McPhee's professional financial records, materials relating to her time as a member of the faculty at the Massachusetts College of Art and Design, project files, other papers and realia (including vintage cameras and flashbulbs), and works by other artists.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 138-139: Restricted until January 1, 2050. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 138-139: Restricted until January 1, 2050. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Massachusetts College of Art and Design

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials relating to Laura McPhee's time as a member of the faculty at the Massachusetts College of Art and Design (MassArt), where she has worked since 1986.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous printed materials published by MassArt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Project files include research, correspondence, and print lists, as well as preparatory and promotion material related to exhibitions of Laura McPhee's images, her books No Ordinary Land (1998), Girls: Ordinary Girls And Their Extraordinary Pursuits (2000), and The Home And The World: A View Of Calcutta (2014), and other publications using her images.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers and receipts relating to Laura McPhee's work photographing the San Andreas Fault in California for a Condé Nast article by Martha McPhee, 1999.
No photographic material is present.

title.part: Desert Chronicle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers and photographs (research material) relating to Laura McPhee's research and travel while working on Desert Chronicle , approximately 2013.

title.part: The Dividing Line

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers and correspondence relating to the companion book for Virginia Beahan and Laura McPhee's exhibit, The Dividing Line: Collaborative Landscape Photography by Virginia Beahan and Laura McPhee , at the Rose Art Museum (Brandeis University) in 1995.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color film negatives (6 x 6 cm and 21 x 26 cm) and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of American painter and sculptor Frank Stella, 1995.
Some images appear in Sidney Guberman's Frank Stella: An Illustrated Biography (New York: Rizzoli International, 1995).

title.part: Girls: Ordinary Girls and Their Extraordinary Pursuits

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers and photographic material created by Laura McPhee during her research and content creation for Girls: Ordinary Girls and Their Extraordinary Pursuits (Random House, 2000), in which she and her sisters Jenny McPhee and Martha McPhee traveled across the United States to collect original photographs and life stories from young women.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Within each grouping, original order is retained.

title.part: Guardians of Solitude

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book proof for Laura McPhee's Guardians of Solitude (Iris Editions, Ltd., 2009), which features images made in three canyons in the White Cloud Mountains of central Idaho in 2008.

title.part: Mothers & Daughters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Press releases and newspaper clippings for Mothers & Daughters: An Exploration In Photographs (Aperture: New York, 1987), which includes images by Laura McPhee and other photographers accompanied by essays by Tillie Olsen and Estelle Jussim.

title.part: No Ordinary Land

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers, correspondence, book proof, and photographs relating to Virginia Beahan and Laura McPhee's research and travel while working on No Ordinary Land: Encounters in a Changing Environment (Aperture: New York, 1998).
As part of the collaborative work, Beahan and McPhee traveled to Hawaii and Iceland (among other locations).
Some materials pertain to the purchase of photographs of lava taken in Hawaii.

did.unittitle: Photographs of Beahan and McPhee in Iceland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color film negatives (6 x 6 cm) and contact proofs for images created by Laura McPhee of Hawaii for Travel & Leisure , 2006. Several images are of her father, John McPhee.
Some images appear in a 2007 article with text by Martha McPhee.

title.part: The Home and the World: A View of Calcutta

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material created in the production of Laura McPhee's The Home and the World: A View of Calcutta (New Haven, Connecticut: Yale University Press, 2014), which features a selection of her works made in and around India's former capital and includes a foreword by Amitav Ghosh and an essay by art historian Romita Ray.

did.unittitle: Mock-up, case binding with book jacket

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three loose, unidentified prints are laid in the front cover.

title.part: River of No Return

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related material: Laura McPhee River of No Return Collection (WA Photos 591), Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and papers relating to Laura McPhee's research and travel while working on River of No Return , 2004-2015.
Also present is a book proof for the project, which documents Idaho's Sawtooth Valley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers and correspondence relating to Laura McPhee's research and travel in Sri Lanka and Kolkata, India. Among the correspondents are members of McPhee's immediate family and photographer Virginia Beahan.
A portion of the material relates specifically to McPhee's work completed while on a Fulbright Scholars Fellowship in 1998.

did.unittitle: "Official papers and miscellaneous: Kolkata, Sri Lanka"
"Laura McPhee: Calcutta Driveway / Idaho-Sorted by Date / Calcutta 2005" (CD)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Film negatives and contact proofs, as well as color prints, created by Laura McPhee for Teen People's September 2001 "global cities trend report."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed maps of the American West. Potentially used by Laura McPhee during her work on Desert Chronicle , Guardians of Solitude , or River of No Return .
Included is one map, in French, of Parc naturel régional de Camargue (Camargue Regional Nature Park).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional material and newspaper clippings related to solo exhibitions, exhibitions with Virginia Beahan, and group exhibitions including images created by Laura McPhee.

title.part: Beyond Wilderness
Calcutta
Guardians of Solitude
Home and the World: A View of Calcutta
Laura Pryde McPhee Photographs
Shadows of White Clouds
Silent Steps
Something About Love
Two Years Later
The Country Between Us
The Dividing Line
Hot Spots
No Ordinary Land
River of No Return
Abstractions and Realities
All in the Family
Altered Eden
America in View
America Now
Becoming Trees
Between You and Me
By the Sea
Home
Landslide
Mimetic Poisoning
Mirage: Energy, Water and Creativity in the Great Basin
The Mural as Muse
Points of View II: Women's Art
The Recorded Image
Upright Female

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: Papers and Realia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes address books, application materials, artists statements, curricula vitae, headshots, unpublished writings, and other papers, as well as two vintage cameras, flashbulbs, and a photo-oil colors master set.

title.part: Art in Action

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proofs from a photoportrait sessions with photographer Abraham Aronow.

did.unittitle: "Papers referring to freelance and shows with the ladies"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Eighty color prints of one image printed across a color wheel. Image is of "Carrie" in a wedding dress holding hands with a child.

did.unittitle: General Electric Mascot exposure light meter (PR-30)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: Art Books by Former Students and Exhibitions and Events.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Art books by Laura McPhee's former students at the Massachusetts College of Art and Design, as well as promotional materials for exhibitions and events not including McPhee's works.

did.unittitle: Arthurs, Ryan. The Height of Land
Morell, Abelardo, Alyssa McDonald, and Irina Rozovsky. Continuum
Rozovsky, Irina. Island in my mind
Spiridonova, Yulia. Neither You Nor I

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 185-188: Restricted until after the death of John McPhee. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains letters between Laura McPhee and family, friends, photographers, and others.
Included are cards featuring original artwork and postcards.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 185-188: Restricted until after the death of John McPhee. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holiday cards addressed to Laura McPhee. All cards include original artwork.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series IV is arranged alphabetically by format or content with photographs filed at the end.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series IV contains address books, high school yearbooks from Solebury School, passports and other forms of identification, photographs, travel ephemera, and other papers kept by Laura McPhee.
Photographs are chiefly of Isobel McPhee, Laura McPhee, and Joan Sullivan, but do include photographs presumably collected by Laura McPhee (including two tintypes and two cabinet cards of children).

did.unittitle: Identification card for Rhode Island School of Design
Miscellaneous materials relating to John McPhee
Miscellaneous materials relating to Pryde Brown

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an envelope addressed to Sarah Ross Finnie (1977), a vehicle registration (1984), two tickets to Stephen Murray at the Royale Theatre (1988), and a ticket to the 2012 Barnard College commencement.
Also present are a pinback button for The Ramones, Lady Washington pearl buttons, the lid of a tea tin, an unopened pack of Walker Evans postcards, and loose papers.

did.unittitle: Photographs (keychain camera from Las Vegas, Nevada)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series V is organized into two subseries: Journals and Magazines and Other Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series V contains printed material using Laura McPhee's images in publication or referencing/reviewing her works.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Journals and magazines featuring the work of Laura McPhee. Some periodicals feature the work of John McPhee, Martha McPhee, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Books and other publications by Laura McPhee or featuring her work.

title.part: The Architecture of Bathing: Body, Landscape, Art
The Creative Arts at Princeton
Critical Messages: Contemporary Northwest Artists on the Environment
Extraction: Art on the Edge of the Abyss
Forces of Change: A New View of Nature
Gateway: Visions for an Urban National Park

Grand Street #70: Against Nature
Here Be Monsters: Poems
